_comment: Guardrail + budget policy for the Command Center orchestrator. JSON, not YAML: stdlib-only parsing (json module), matching the fleet_bus.py 'pure stdlib, runs on every fleet machine' convention. Reviewable by the operator; the loop reads this every cycle, never hardcodes these numbers in code.
_note_spawn_worker: spawn_worker is green ONLY while under concurrency_cap AND under the budget warn threshold below — guardrail.py enforces this, it is not a blanket green. Exceeding either reclassifies the same action as spawn_beyond_cap (red).

budget._note_max_cycles: Safety ceiling, ~2x the normal rate. At cycle_interval_seconds=1800 the loop runs 48 cycles/day, i.e. steady-state 50% budget. This was 48 originally — exactly equal to the daily cycle count, so the budget guardrail would have false-alarmed at 100% every single day (caught 2026-07-12 during the v2 review, before the always-on install).

spawn._comment: v2 (2026-07-12): real worker spawning, guardrailed. Every launch clears ALL of: mode gate, concurrency cap (budget.max_concurrent_workers), max_spawns_per_day, prior-art gate, budget ceiling, HALT — or it is refused (fail-closed). lib/spawn.py enforces; nothing here is hardcoded in code.
spawn._note_mode: propose = every spawn is proposed to the operator (via cc-master --to human) and waits for his explicit 'go' before launching; auto = green spawns launch within caps without per-spawn gating. Graduate propose->auto ONLY on the operator's explicit say-so — mirrors how v1 shipped decision-only first. Flipping this to 'auto' is the live-autonomy switch; do not flip it in a build session.
spawn._note_max_spawns_per_day: Counted from ledger 'spawn' rows with today's date. A hard daily launch ceiling independent of concurrency (a worker can finish and free a slot many times/day).
spawn._note_worker_limits: Per-worker hard caps: --max-turns bounds tool-loop length, timeout SIGTERMs a worker that overruns. These are the always-available proxy for token budget (real $ metering below is best-effort/machine-local).

inference._comment: Cheap, no Claude budget, no tool loop — summarize/classify/extract/draft/triage subtasks. Routed per fleet/dispatch.md (Ollama :11434 fleet-wide, Gemini CLI, NVIDIA NIM). Fleet-first: prefer this tier for any task it can do well enough.

claude-worker._comment: Agentic build/debug/multi-tool work — Sonnet per the operator's model-routing rules. Runs local as a subprocess on the host, or via `ssh <machine> claude -p` for a repo-owning machine (both, per-task — the operator 2026-07-12). commit-local-never-push: the worker settings.json denies all push/merge/deploy/delete; the master surfaces the worker's local branch for human review.

budget_usd._comment: Real $ ceiling, best-effort. The proxy caps above (max_spawns_per_day, max_turns, timeout, max_concurrent_workers) are the always-available HARD ceiling. usage_state_path is read when present on the host for a true spend number surfaced on the dashboard; it is machine-local and absent on some hosts, in which case the proxy caps alone govern — never silently faked as equivalent (same honesty as ledger.py's cycle-count budget note).

denied_permissions_note: Enforced at the settings.json permission layer for the orchestrator's own Claude session, see ../settings.orchestrator.json — this policy file governs the deterministic loop's own action classification, the settings.json is the hard technical backstop for anything that DOES invoke Claude.
